page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg6.4xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY processor. It features 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at a base frequency of 3.1 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance operates on the KVM hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance consistently in the average tier. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1284.0 and a multi-core score of 7869.0, alongside a PassMark CPU score of 16805.51. Memory bandwidth tests show 855.0 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 90.64 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. For specialized workloads, the instance handles Redis SET operations at 15.39 million operations per second and static web serving at 12.4 GB/sec.

While the dedicated Intel Xeon processor provides stable compute performance, the instance exhibits a performance bottleneck in larger machine learning workloads. For instance, LLM inference prompt processing with a medium 7B model drops to a weak tier at 23.22 tokens per second, and large 70B models are unsupported due to memory and hardware constraints. Consequently, this instance is best suited for general-purpose applications, software compilation, database operations, and moderate web serving rather than heavy deep learning tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg6.4xlarge is a general-purpose, KVM-hypervised server instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with a dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY processor offering 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz, alongside 64.0 GB of system memory. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Benchmark results position the server in the average tier across most metrics, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 7869.0 and a PassMark CPU score of 16805.51. While it handles Redis SET operations at 15.39 million operations per second and static web serving at 12.4 GB/sec, it exhibits weak performance in medium-sized LLM inference tasks. This hardware configuration is best suited for software compilation, database workloads, and general-purpose web serving.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg6.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs at 3.1 GHz, 64 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, suitable for database operations and software compilation.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg6.4xlarge is a KVM-based instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated Intel-powered instance offering balanced compute and memory resources for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, and database operations.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY vCPUs running at 3.1 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage
Average performance in software compilation and database operations
Weak performance for medium-sized LLM inference tasks
Best suited for general-purpose web serving and application hosting
